title: Post-Delivery Dispute with Conflicting Digital Footprint Evidence
narrative: Cardholder purchases $180-$450 retail items (ProductCD='W' apparel or electronics) with standard shipping, then files 'item not received' chargeback 45-75 days post-transaction despite delivery confirmation. Pattern shows legitimate purchase behavior initially: matching billing/shipping addresses (dist1=0), email domain matches historical profile (P_emaildomain consistent with card1 history), transaction during normal hours (txn_hour 10-20), and card1_txn_count_7day=1-3 indicating non-velocity play. However, dispute timing clusters suspiciously at 73-78 days (approaching chargeback deadline window), and cardholder often has 2-4 prior 'not received' claims across different merchants within 180-day window. Cross-merchant analysis reveals identical shipping address (addr1) used successfully for 8-12 other deliveries during same period without dispute, creating forensic contradiction.
indicators: TransactionAmt between $180-$450 with ProductCD='W' and standard shipping method
dist1=0 indicating matching billing and shipping addresses at purchase time
Chargeback filed 73-78 days post-transaction, near dispute deadline window
Cardholder history shows 2-4 'item not received' disputes across merchants within 180 days
Same addr1 received 8-12 successful deliveries from other merchants during dispute period
card1_txn_count_7day=1-3 and txn_hour between 10-20, suggesting legitimate purchase pattern
P_emaildomain consistency with card1 historical profile, no account takeover indicators
summary: A cardholder makes a legitimate-looking purchase with matching billing and shipping addresses, receives the item, then files an 'item not received' chargeback 73-78 days later—right before the dispute deadline expires. The same shipping address successfully received 8-12 other deliveries during this period without any issues.
reasoning: The cardholder is exploiting chargeback protections to get a refund while keeping the merchandise. The strategic timing near the deadline and contradictory delivery history at the same address reveal intentional false claims rather than genuine delivery problems.
indicator_explanations: Mid-range purchase with physical goods and regular delivery chosen
Billing and shipping addresses match at checkout, no mismatch flag
Dispute filed close to the deadline window, classic strategic timing
Customer has pattern of claiming packages never arrived across multiple stores
Same address successfully received many other deliveries during the complaint timeframe
Purchase timing and frequency look normal, not suspicious transaction behavior
Email account is stable and consistent, rules out account compromise
